title: Who We Are
orientation: An organizational 'about' page introducing Abolitionists Rising, the group's stated mission, theological priorities, and the activist tactics they use to fight abortion. It is aimed at first-time visitors trying to figure out what the organization is and how it differs from the broader pro-life movement.
audiences: Journalist or researcher profiling the movement

questions.question: What is Abolitionists Rising and what is its mission?
questions.answer: Abolitionists Rising is an organization that promotes abolitionist ideas and supports abolitionist organizations, projects, and legislation. Its three stated goals are the abolition of abortion, the repentance of the nation, and the glorification of God.
questions.quote: Abolitionists Rising... ...exists to spread abolitionist ideas and support abolitionist organizations, projects, and legislation. our goals are the abolition of abortion, the repentance of the nation, and the glorification of God.

questions.question: How does Abolitionists Rising actually spread its message?
questions.audience: Journalist or researcher profiling the movement
questions.answer: The group produces and distributes both digital content and printed materials intended to counter what it considers misinformation and arguments against the knowledge of God. These resources are sold through an online store linked from the site.
questions.quote: We spread abolitionist ideas through the creation and dissemination of digital content and print materials which destroy misinformation and lofty arguments raised against the knowledge of God.

questions.question: How can I find an abolitionist group near me?
questions.answer: The site hosts an interactive map that promotes abolitionist organizations across the country and is intended to connect visitors with the group nearest to them.
questions.quote: We support abolitionist organizations by promoting them on this website. It is our hope that every visitor clicks on our interactive map and gets plugged in to the abolitionist organization nearest them.

questions.question: How does the group support abolitionist legislation?
questions.answer: Abolitionists Rising uses action alerts on its interactive map to tell supporters how to back specific abolitionist bills, and it views growing the movement itself as another form of legislative support.
questions.quote: We support abolitionist legislation through action alerts on the interactive map which instruct people how to support abolitionist bills around the country. We also support abolitionist legislation by growing the Abolitionist Movement.

questions.question: What does Abolitionists Rising believe is the top priority of the movement?
questions.answer: The organization frames the glory of God and obedience to Him as the first priority of the movement, treating any other goal as secondary no matter how worthy it might seem.
questions.quote: The chief end of man is to glorify God and enjoy Him forever. Thus, the glory of God and obedience to Him is the first priority of the Abolitionist Movement. No goal, no matter how righteous, must ever supplant our chief desire for God to be glorified.

questions.question: Does Abolitionists Rising support incremental anti-abortion laws?
questions.answer: No. The group rejects incremental legislation that regulates the circumstances of legal abortion and instead advocates for laws that grant equal legal protection to preborn children and treat abortion as criminal homicide.
questions.quote: We seek to abolish abortion totally and immediately. We do not call for incremental legislation that regulates the who, what, when, where, and why of legal abortion. We call for abolitionist legislation that makes preborn children equal under law and criminalizes abortion as murder.

questions.question: Why does Abolitionists Rising call for national repentance, not just policy change?
questions.answer: The group sees abortion as a symptom of national sin, and argues that policy alone cannot fix it. Repentance, revival, and reformation are presented as the only adequate response for a country that, in their words, kills its own children.
questions.quote: We call the nation to repentance because repentance is the only solution to sin. Repentance is the only way out of the depravity and insanity that the United States has descended to. In a nation that hates and murders its own children, nothing short of repentance, revival, and wholesale reformation will do.

questions.question: Is Abolitionists Rising part of the pro-life movement?
questions.answer: No. The organization explicitly distances itself from the pro-life movement, claiming differences in theology, tactics, and goals, and points readers to its 'Abolitionism 101' resource for a fuller explanation.
questions.quote: Abolitionists Rising is not part of the Pro-Life Movement. We are separate from the Pro-Life Movement in theology, tactics, and goals.

related.title: Abolitionism 101
related.reason: Directly linked from the about page as the primary explainer for the movement's distinctives.

related.title: Abolitionist, Not Pro-Life
related.reason: Expands on the about page's claim that abolitionism is separate from the pro-life movement.

related.title: Christian, Not Pro-Life
related.reason: Companion argument framing the same theological objection to the pro-life label.

related.title: Five Reasons Abolitionists Oppose Incremental Pro-Life Bills
related.reason: Backs up the about page's rejection of incremental legislation.

related.title: Why Do Pro-Lifers Despise Abolitionists?
related.reason: Explores the friction between the two movements that the about page asserts exists.

related.title: Fruits of Abolitionism: Is True Repentance Necessary?
related.reason: Develops the about page's theme of national repentance as essential to the movement.

related.title: Abolitionism Spreading to Campuses Across the Nation
related.reason: Concrete example of the organizational growth and on-the-ground activism the about page promotes.
